speaker: interviewer
text: I guess my question is, what kept the hope going?

speaker: interviewee
text: [SIGHS] Well, what kept the hope going is also I wanted to [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] to see--to see my family again. [PAUSES FOR 8 SECONDS] You know, and to go home and see my family. Be with my family. [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewee
text: But the only one that remained alive is my brother. [PAUSES FOR 15 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: Anything else you want to say before we look at the photographs? [SIGHS] [PAUSES FOR 13 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewee
text: I don't know. [PAUSES FOR 12 SECONDS] Well, just that all-in-all, [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] being on my own [PAUSES FOR 6 SECONDS] at such a young age, [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] so I feel like I did pretty good for myself. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] You know, I did everything on my own. Nobody to help me.

speaker: interviewee
text: Thank you. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] And I have lots of friends. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] As you know, I'm involved in international folk dancing. And I like ballroom dancing, also. And-- and most of the time, [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] I have lady friends, and I'm in a relationship. Sometimes it lasts three years, sometimes two years, sometimes one year. [LAUGHS] But I never remarried.

speaker: interviewee
text: I think it was said 1963 or '64. [PAUSES FOR 6 SECONDS] And I'm in very good relationship with my ex-wife's family. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] Whenever there is a-- you know, a party, or they-- or a wedding, or a bar mitzvah, or something, I'm always invited. And they still think of me as family. And I think of them as family.

speaker: interviewee
text: Thank you. This is myself. It was taken in Budapest after the war. It's my first picture after the war that was taken.

speaker: interviewee
text: In 1945. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] The month, I don't remember exactly. July or August. OK, this is a picture that was taken. I was on my way to the United States. The badge that I have-- that I'm wearing is the badge that was given to us from the organization that brought me to the United States, called US Committee.

speaker: interviewer
text: And what year was that picture taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is a picture that was taken in Germany from the group of children that came to the United States from this same organization, the U.S. Committee's children organization. And we all came to the United States together, [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] until New York. And from there on, some of them went to different places from there.

speaker: interviewee
text: This is a picture that was taken in Austria, just before I left Austria for the United States. And this is a group of my friends. We lived in a displaced persons camp in Austria, in Linsk.

speaker: interviewee
text: That was in-- [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] it could have been the-- the early part of '46. I don't remember exactly.

speaker: interviewee
text: 1946, yes. This is my brother and his wife on his wedding. A wedding picture with her family and his friends.

speaker: interviewer
text: And when was the picture taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: That picture was taken, I believe, in 1956.

speaker: interviewee
text: This is my daughter, my-- and my son-in-law, and my grandson.

speaker: interviewee
text: My daughter's name is Karen. My son-in-law's name is Allen, Al. And my grandson is Jason Allen Lovoy.

speaker: interviewer
text: And where was the picture taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: In Los Angeles. I don't know exactly where or when.

speaker: interviewee
text: More or less, probably two years. About a year ago.

speaker: interviewee
text: As I was saying, I do international folk dancing for a hobby. And this is my folk dance friends. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] So we were having a festival. So we took some pictures just before the festival.

speaker: interviewer
text: And when was that festival? When was the picture taken, more or less?

speaker: interviewee
text: More or less. Probably about four years ago, I would say.

speaker: interviewee
text: That was taken at the-- in the back where the Culver Memorial Auditorium is.
